church_of_plenty.quote: Glory be to those who share with those who cannot.
church_of_plenty.description: A religious party of the Ascendancy, the Church of Plenty is dedicated to the people, the land, and a simplistic lifestyle.

bulletin_message.00:
"I think it is time we spoke about the "tiefling in the room", so to speak..."

The horned red lady looks uncomfortable.

open_letter.bulletin_title: Your guide looks uneasily at you all
open_letter.choice_000: I... Yes, we must discuss this.
open_letter.choice_001: [skip] Can we talk about it later?

bulletin_message.00: "Yes it is true I am a tiefling. A being of supposedly demonic blood. But still the church allowed me entry. The Church of Plenty, your patrons in this expedition, allow all creeds and all races to serve and be served by the church. I have taken the clerical oaths and risen to 'priestess'. Now it is my job to accompany beginning congregations on their journey into the world."

read_letter.choice_000: We all serve the church how we can, we understand.

bulletin_message.00: "Like yourselves, I once set out to found a town with another congregation, but as a settler. The monks I accompanied shunned and tormented me, but I stayed true to the teachings of the church. It is not in my nature to find anger toward others. The Elders heard of my mistreatment and sent a group of Knights of Plenty to escort me safely back to the monastery. It was then I decided I wouldn't let anyone else be treated like that, and dove deeper into my studies."

read_letter2.choice_000: How terrible! Our town will not be like that!

bulletin_message.00: "I am glad to hear that. But first before you can properly set up, you need to decide which of the tenets of the church you will most closely follow, and I will make the changes to your holy reliquary to represent those." she gestures at the large chicken statue a few of you had taken it in turns to carry, before beginning again."

"You have the Mark of Care. A dedication to everything around you, man and nature. Next is the Mark of Might. Strength and the earth is your magnificent bountiful resource. Lastly, the Mark of Prosperity. No congregation can survive without growth. Prosperity is a sure way to achieve that."

"You will make a fine congregation and church outpost" Orianna says.

"[str(i18n_data.town_name)]" will prosper if you all work hard and look after one another. "Do not forget the tenets of the church and show respect to all things. Be pleased, young outpost"
"Now I must return to the capitol, I have other duties the church requires of me. Look for me in the coming weeks, for I will return to praise the church with you once more."

"This food shall sustain you for a time. But please don't forget you will need to look after yourselves still." Orianna smiles at the group she has accompanied. "Take this and prosper, for the church."

The bag contains:
I'm back! Oh my, look at this place! Well then, I think you are definitely ready... But that can wait. First, catch me up on what has been going on!

msg1.choice_000: Welcome back! So here's the story...

bulletin_message.00: Orianna hangs on every word, only speaking when you are finished. "Wow that sure sounds like an adventure of ups and downs! I'm glad you're all okay!" She gives you all a reassuring smile "Anyway, as I was saying before, it seems your little community is ready for the next step.".

...and begins. "This place you have settled, it is just one of many across Hearth where fledgling communities like your own are beginning, and unfortunately, some ending. I was talking to my friend and nephew, Trissk, the other day and he told me about how the civilizations all around us, even our own Ascendancy, make changes to their community hearths to mark a "specialty"  or unique..ity..? nes..? well, you get the idea..." She grins at you in such a childish way, you forget she is a fully grown adult for a second.

"I think, if you can manage to upgrade your hearth like those others, then you'll stand a better chance of surviving whatever unholy abominations" she shivers at the mention"may come to harm you. Oh, however I am sorry, friends. I must go, I hear the cries of wounded nearby." Orianna bolts from the fireside, accidentally losing a few vials of a clear liquid from a belt pouch.
Or, our minds can envision a Hearth of Makers, while not particularly suited to our own ascetic lifestyle, our crafters would be able to create pieces that would make the Ascendancy thrive.
Now that my little friend has helped you discover the wonders of hearth magic, it is time you made a decision. Which hearth would you like to work towards?

"Uh, yes. Hello, I am Trissk. It is a pleasure to meet you all. And no, I am not a kobold, I am a lizardfolk. We are always mistaken for those monsters"

He takes a moment to recompose himself before he continues.

"Now, about the hearths. From my travels I have devised plans for three different choices."
"The Hearth of Cheer is a unique monument erected by only the happiest and most.. well.. cheerful of congregations. It is, however, well within reach for almost everyone!"
The three choices I managed to plan out are as follows. The hearth of Cheer, The Hearth of Faith, and the Hearth of Makers.

Lastly, a Hearth of Faith can be realized when our dreams of protection and dedication to the church are realized. This one is my own design, and I am yet to see anything like it in all my travels. I believe this is the one that most distinctly and faithfully represents the church.

"Greetings friends! I would like to introduce the fine congregation of "[str(i18n_data.town_name)]" to a friend of mine. I have metioned him before but this time he decided to travel with me to meet you all. Everyone, meet Trissk. Being the more learned one, he will explain the magics of hearths to you."

faith.name: Hearth of Faith
faith.description:
You have proven your faith, and continue to do so. The church looks upon you warmly.
  • Your hearthlings will benefit from an increased spirit stat.
  • Knowledge and wisdom come easier to the members of your congregation.

bulletin_message.00: Orianna returns once more to shepherd your congregation, this time without Trissk in tow. "You have grown again, I can feel your strong spirits. This is good."

She looks distracted by something. "It pains me to bring you this news, but our congregations across the land are dwindling. Some have succumb to the dangers of settling, some have found their way back to the capitol to tell tales of large monsters and a flying squid-like thing. That is why I am here. I wanted to make sure you were all okay. I'm on my way back to the capitol now, but in the meantime, I think it is almost time you erect a grand monument to commemorate your successful township. "
"I would suggest the shrine represent one of the core virtues of the church. Protection, care and life, or the wealth many leave untapped, knowledge."

msg3.choice_000: But how do we do this?

bulletin_message.00:
"Well, you will have to wait for me to return with some special guests, who can teach you of the Shrine making ways."

Orianna rises, thanking you for just being, before setting out again, toward the capitol...

Orianna arrives with a group of Knights of Plenty and a few clerics. Beside her walks the small lizard, Trissk, and on the other side, a man you all recognize as the Ascendancy embarkment liason, Mer Burlyhands. "Congregation of [str(i18n_data.town_name)], I bring to you Mer Burlyhands, chied liason to newly embarking towns of the Ascendancy."

"Hello all! My, it's not often I get out into the field, and even more rare still that I am granted a church escort! He looks behind himself at the squad of knights and clerics"

Now, I hope you don't mind but I brought some friends of mine alone who have been crucial in Ascendancy settlement shrines right across the land. They'll take turns explaining how to construct a Shrine.

Ember of the Church steps forward, her eyes gleaming as she stands by Orianna.
"It is with great joy that I am granted leave of the monastery to travel the land to a settlement of our own. The church rejoices in your success. I wish to propose we build a Cornucopia of Life. To help keep you all in the long journey ahead. "
(Building a Cornucopia of Life will grant all your townsfolk a +1 boost to the mind, body, and spirit attributes.)
"The Cornucopia would be a beautiful monument!"

"Ahem, I do believe" begins Marak, guildmaster of the Ascendancy. "That your resources would be best suited for the Font of Knowledge. Just imagine, a bookshelf in a fountain, so engineered that the books never get wet!"
(Building a Font of Knowledge will grant lost wisdom, allowing the creation of Masterwork quality crafted goods.)
"Ah, yes, about him... Why don't we move on?"

"Greetings, I am Anwyn Toressi, of the royal guard. When I set out to accompany Mer Burlyhands here, it was not my intention to discuss shrines with you fine church-faring folk, but after watching the church's Knights and clerics on the way here, I can tell there may be some fight in you, after all!"

"Yes after seeing them in action I can only be reminded of the fabled Keeper of Cid. Few know of the existence of the keeper, although they are said to have been a Paladin, if you can believe it. The guard and I actually confiscated some stolen plans for some mighty powerful weapons and armor said to have been worn by Cid's Keeper, perhaps if you memorialized the unknown individual in some way, the city and church would allow me to share such plans with you!"
(Building a Shrine to Cids Keeper will grant you knowledge of exceptionally powerful equipment.)

"May this congregation forever survive the annuls of time, and transcend to greater heights in the heirachy of the church. I consider you all to be my greatest of friends, and I wish you all the best! I must be getting Lord Burlyhands back to the capitol now, however I think a few of us are planning to stay and see your shrine come to fruition. Be well, friends!"

The people gather together to choose their Destiny...

unlock_shrine_recipes.bulletin_title: Mason unlocked Font of Knowledge recipe!

unlock_shrine_recipes3.bulletin_title: Cook unlocked Cornucopia of Life recipe!

valor.name: Cids Keeper
valor.description:
This Township shall be dedicated to Cid's valorous example, when he protected pilgrims on the road to found the Ascendant Empire.
  • Unlocks Legendary weapon recipes
  • Unlocks castle decoration recipes

guildmaster.name: Font of Knowledge
guildmaster.description:
This Township shall be known for its artisans' pursuit of wisdom and magical crafting.
  • Allows crafting Masterwork quality items
  • Unlocks artisan-themed decoration recipes

deity.name: Cornucopia of Life
deity.description:
This Township emulates kindness and love, and a respect for all nature like no other.
  • Blesses all citizens with +1 to Mind, Body, and Spirit
  • Unlocks nature-themed decoration recipes

shrine_upgrade.title: Township Established
shrine_upgrade.reward_message: By the will of the church, we recognize:
shrine_upgrade.reward_message_ctd: For harnessing the powers of the faith. The congregation is hereby eligible for:

bulletin_message.00: Ember stands, with a big warm grin you have become accustomed to seeing on visitors faces.
"The Cornucopia looks magnificent!" Ember muses. "This is a fine monument,indeed! but I could never expect anything less from a congregation of the Plenty.

"May the bounty of this horn never run dry! However, it is with great regret that I fear I must leave you now. The work of a missionary is never done and I hear there is an Ascendancy town near here that is also looking to take the next step! Be pleased, young flock. Be pleased." She turns and signals to three of the knights to accompany her as she rides off in a direction you could swear you had seen smoke coming from just days before...
Marak looks disappointed by your shrine, "I said a waterfall cascading over a bookshelf... That was going to be spectacular... Instead all I got was... well, this..."
"Sorry? Is that all? My crow- our crowning achievement of a lifetime... missed by inches... Oh well. Perhaps the Nordlings will be able to do this!"
Marak leaves without another word, leaving you all confused and a little startled at his reaction as some of you tried explaining the mechanics of water and wood around paper...

Anwyn stares at the statue commemorating the unnamed guardian of hearths prophet. "The stones on this Shrine are glowing bright, another guardian recognises you and will no doubt seek to protect you."

Anwyn looks toward the blacksmith and grins. "I am sorry for the small deception, I have been trying to get them to commemorate this fine warrior int he capitol for years but noone ever listened. The plans aren't really a secret, standard church requisition... You could have jsut asked them." she looks at you all sheepishly

msg2.choice_000: You could have just asked, too

bulletin_message.00: Anwyn looks even more sheepish than before. " Yes I do apologize. This is something I am quite passionate about, and I knew I could only rely on people of the church to bring it to life. Once more, I am very sorry for the deception, and here are the plans. I will leave you now to return to the capitol, I fear I have been gone too long."

Orianna jumps around and hugs you all "Well done children of the church! Well done flock of [str(i18n_data.town_name)], You have achieved so much! The church thanks you a million times over and I am glad I have made so many new friends!"

msg1.choice_000: We are glad to know you

bulletin_message.00: "Perhaps the church will allow me leave to come visit every now and then still, I know you don't exactly need the help anymore, now that you are a fully fledged congregation in your own right, but it would still be nice to see my friends."

msg2.choice_000: This is our home, and yours any time!

bulletin_message.00: " Oh this has been such a pleasant experience and I have loved spending all this time with you all! Unfortunately I must go, I am needed elsewhere... I will miss you all dearly, Be pleased, everyone."

"You know... The church is always looking to expand their horizons and the horizons of the Ascendancy. If there were some of you wishing to start out fresh, perhaps you would have a better chance of making it big again..."

Re-embarking allows you to carry over up to 3 citizens, 10 items, and all your unlocked recipes when starting a new town.

hearth_upgrade_faith.title: Settlement Established
hearth_upgrade_faith.reward_message: By unanimous agreement of the citizens, we declare:
hearth_upgrade_faith.reward_message_ctd: To be a settlement out to prove that it has unrivaled faith in the church of plenty.
